issues.title: The template string is not valid.

comments.body:
This occurs because `ChatClient` ultimately calls a `PromptTemplate` constructor which attempts to use ST. 

This makes it difficult to use `ChatClient` with another, more capable, templating mechanism like Jinjava or Mustache.  It's particularly problematic when emitting code examples, which is a common requirement. See [#1849. ](https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-ai/issues/1849). I've been forced several times to use `ChatModel` rather than `ChatClient` to avoid this problem, which is disappointing as it doesn't provide elegant `entity` conversion.

**It should be possible to pass a literal String that won't be evaluated as a template if the user chooses.**

This simple change might allow users to use their own template mechanism, potentially deferring the need to [support other template mechanisms](https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-ai/issues/1687).

This has now been fixed, the PR is https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-ai/pull/2920 and searching for TemplateRenderer in the docs will show you how to use it. You can provide your own TemplateRender impls.
